messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online course: Clustering High-Dimensional Data with R/Python
messages.subject_clean: Online course: Clustering High-Dimensional Data with R/Python
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-ECOLOGY members

Explore advanced techniques for analyzing complex datasets and extracting meaningful insights from your research. Clustering High-Dimensional Data 3.0 is a 2-day seminar livestreaming May 26, led by Nikolay Oskolkov (Lund University). Researchers in ecology and environmental science often encounter complex, high-dimensional datasets from various sources like vegetation, soils, and hydrology. This seminar provides practical skills in clustering methods using R and Python, enabling you to identify hidden patterns and group similar data points within your own ecological data. By mastering advanced clustering techniques and dimensionality reduction, you can translate complex datasets into meaningful scientific insights for your specific research questions.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/clustering-high-dimensional-data-30) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] online course: Interactive Maps with R
messages.subject_clean: online course: Interactive Maps with R
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are pleased to announce our upcoming online course: Interactive Maps with R

Dates: 13?14 May 


Interactive maps are powerful tools for exploring and communicating spatial data. They allow users to zoom, pan, switch layers, and interact with geographic information in an intuitive way, and are increasingly used in research and communication.
 
In this hands-on course, you will learn how to build and customize interactive maps in R packages such as leaflet and mapview, working with both your own and external spatial data.
 
You will gain practical experience in creating, customizing, and publishing interactive maps ready for sharing with collaborators or the public.
 
What attendees will learn:

? Building interactive multi-layer maps in R
? Adding pop-ups, labels, legends, and controls
? Working with spatial data (your own and external)
? Clustering, insets, and map customization
? Sharing and publishing maps online
 
Feel free to share with colleagues who may be interested.
Best regards,

Carlo
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Last chance: Online course: Exploratory Multivariate Analysis with R
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Online course: Exploratory Multivariate Analysis with R
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-ECOLOGY members

Last chance - Discover practical R-based strategies for robust empirical research and clearer findings. Exploratory Multivariate Analysis in R is a 2-day seminar livestreaming May 7 and 20 with SMCS (Universit? Catholique de Louvain). This seminar introduces exploratory multivariate analysis with a focus on practical implementation in R for empirical research. For ecologists and environmental scientists, these methods enable dimensionality reduction, discovery of latent structures, and handling of mixed data types common in vegetation, soil, or hydrological studies. Participants will gain skills to apply robust R-based workflows, improving inference and the clarity of published results in their own research.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/exploratory-multivariate-analysis-in-r) to register now, and we'd appreciate you sharing this with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Generalised Linear Models (GLMs) in R
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

You can still join us and learn how to use Generalised Linear Models (GLMs) as a single, flexible framework to analyse many types of data in R (continuous, binary, and count data).
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/glm-in-r-1/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/glm-in-r-1/ ) 
 
Attendees will learn how to:

Fit and interpret GLMs in R

Choose the right model for your data

Understand categorical and continuous predictors

Perform model selection

Visualise and communicate results
 
Related courses:
 
[ Generalized Linear Mixed Models in R ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/glmms-in-r/ ) - ONLINE, 21-24 September 
 
[ Generalised Additive Models in R ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gams-in-r/ ) - ONLINE,  7-10 December
 
Best regards,
Carlo


--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Last chance: Online course: Modelling and Mapping Trait Evolution
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Online course: Modelling and Mapping Trait Evolution
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-ECOLOGY members

Last chance - This seminar offers practical skills for researchers using R to analyze evolutionary patterns in their data. Modelling and Mapping Trait Evolution is a 2-day seminar livestreaming May 11 and 13 with Renan Maestri (Department of Ecology, Universidade Federal do Rio Grande do Sul). This seminar offers statistical and computational methods for modelling and mapping trait evolution on phylogenies, integrating theoretical foundations with practical R implementations. Researchers in ecology and environmental science can apply these advanced phylogenetic comparative methods to rigorously test macroevolutionary hypotheses and infer evolutionary processes from their own ecological datasets. Participants will gain practical R workflows using packages like ape, geiger, phytools, and OUwie to produce publication-ready visualisations and strengthen their quantitative ecological research.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/modelling-and-mapping-trait-evolution) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
